max_assumptions_before_block.description: Maximum number of assumptions allowed before blocking. If you make more than this many assumptions, the request should be clarified.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum confidence level required to proceed. Lower confidence triggers HITL intervention.

max_uncertainty_areas.description: Maximum number of uncertainty areas allowed before requiring clarification.

thresholds.description: Gate thresholds — these rules determine if the response can proceed or requires intervention

is_actionable.description: Can the request be acted upon? False if request is too vague, contradictory, or missing critical requirements.

clarity.description: How clear is the request? 'clear' = well-defined, 'partial' = some gaps, 'ambiguous' = cannot determine intent.

missing_requirements.description: What information or requirements are missing that would be needed to proceed?

blockers.description: Explicit show-stoppers preventing any work (contradictions, impossibilities, undefined scope).

assumptions_count.description: Number of assumptions made during work. 0 if no work was attempted.

confidence_level.description: Confidence in the response. 'low' if request is ambiguous or blockers exist.

uncertainty_count.description: Number of areas where outcome is uncertain.

assumptions_list.description: List of assumptions made during work.

uncertainty_areas.description: List of areas with uncertain outcomes.

reported_values.description: Values reported by LLM for gate evaluation

description: Gate rules with evaluation thresholds and LLM-reported values. Thresholds are visible to the LLM in schema descriptions so it knows the evaluation criteria. The CLI compares reported_values against thresholds to make the gate decision.
